---
species: "Gracilaria tikvahiae"
accepted_code: "GRTI"
alternate_codes: # "CODE" for single or ["CODE1", "CODE2"] for >1
diminutive: #"CODE" # uncomment and fill in if one exists
alternate_diminutives: # "CODE" for single or ["CODE1", "CODE2"] for >1
common_name: "graceful redweed"
field_id_possible:
  - yes
description: "Red algae with thick red rubes, often branching from central rib, tips have sharp apex" # make sure to fill this in!
image: '/header_images/Gracilaria_tikvahiae_flickr_JamesStJohn.jpg'
image_credit: 'Photo by James St John on [flickr](https://www.flickr.com/photos/jsjgeology/23689886804)' # MUST have an image credit and link to webpage NOT just the image
categories:
  - erect red algae
sampling_programs:
    - bhi_quadrat
    - bhi_rov
    - bhi_bruv
    - bhi_farms
    - lsw_quad
    - lsw_microquad
habitat:
  - intertidal
  - subtidal
mobility:
  - sessile
sampling_region:
  - GOM
  - Virginian
catami_code: 80300903
catami_version: "1.4"
worms_code: 157354
---


**Other images:** <!-- Other images with attribution -->
<br/>

**Distinguishing features:** Thick red tubes. Branching from central rib. Tips have a sharp apex.
<!-- size, gross morphology, a key identifier, zonation notes (intertidal or depth) -->
<br/>

**Easily confused species and differentiation:** *Cystoclonium purpureum* (CYPU) and *Scytosiphon lomentaria* (SCLO). CYPU has large cylindrical branches that are numerous and alternating. CYPU can be either bristly, tapered branches OR long, twisting tendrils. SCLO is clumps of hollow and unbranced reddish-brown tubes in the lower intertidal.

<br/>

**Extended Description:** <!-- Extended description -->

**[WORMS page](https://marinespecies.org/aphia.php?p=taxdetails&id=157354)**
